deficits.missing: The 'Toxicity Filtering' subsection is EXPLAINED but not BUILDABLE: it describes scoring with 'a lightweight classifier' and Perspective API but ships no runnable classifier. Every other stage in the chapter (LangID, heuristics, quality classifier, PII, exact/Bloom/MinHash dedup) has working code, so toxicity is the one soft spot against the 'harmful-content filtering' deliverable.
deficits.fix: Add a compact runnable toxicity classifier mirroring the quality-classifier block: fasttext.train_supervised on a labeled hate-speech dataset (or a HashingVectorizer+LogisticRegression head) plus a threshold-selection snippet, so harmful-content filtering is implementable from the book alone rather than via an external API.

deficits.missing: Suffix-array exact-substring dedup (Lee et al.) is EXPLAINED with the SA-IS/LCP algorithm sketch and correct 800GB resource accounting but no code, unlike MinHash which is fully implemented from scratch. A reader could not build the substring deduper from the text alone.
deficits.fix: Either add a small from-scratch suffix-array + LCP demo over a toy token sequence with expected repeated-span output, or explicitly scope substring dedup as 'use deduplicate-text-datasets' and mark it out of the buildable set (document-level MinHash already satisfies the fuzzy-dedup deliverable).

deficits.missing: PII redaction and heuristic-filter code lack the deterministic unit-test-with-expected-output treatment that the WET parser, TokenShard, Bloom filter, and MinHash demo all receive; redact_pii and passes_heuristic_filters have only inline comment examples.
deficits.fix: Add a tiny assert-based test block for redact_pii (each PII type -> placeholder) and passes_heuristic_filters (one pass / one fail per rule) to match the chapter's otherwise-excellent verification standard.

summary: Reference-grade coverage of all four A4 deliverables across two chapters (3.1 sourcing/WARC extraction, 3.2 cleaning/dedup/measurement). WARC->text extraction ships working warcio+trafilatura code with charset fallback plus a didactic WET parser with offline tests; LangID uses real fastText lid.176 code with thresholds; quality filtering has full compute_heuristics/passes_heuristic_filters plus a trainable CCNet-style HashingVectorizer+LogisticRegression classifier with percentile thresholding; exact dedup (SHA-256), Bloom-filter dedup (correct m/n=28.8, k=20 sizing with empirical FP verification), and fuzzy MinHash-LSH are all implemented from scratch with shingling, a universal hash family, band/row derivation (t*=(1/b)^(1/r)), a deterministic demo with expected Jaccard outputs, and a 3-sigma estimator check. The fourth deliverable -- measuring downstream filtering effect -- is a fully runnable GPT-2 ablation harness (pack/train/heldout_ppl/acc_norm) with a same-data noise-floor control, hardware-tiered expected magnitudes, and a pointer to lm-eval-harness. Includes resource accounting (TB/GB/hours, 800GB suffix array), failure modes (toxicity bias, benchmark contamination, dedup false positives), and pointers into datatrove/dolma/dclm/deduplicate-text-datasets. All spot-checked numerics (Bloom sizing, LSH candidate probabilities, epoch counts, suffix-array memory) verified correct. Deficits are minor: no runnable toxicity classifier, no suffix-array code, and two stages lacking the chapter's own test-with-expected-value standard. No correctness bugs found; the earlier trafilatura/WET factual error and perplexity-formula ambiguity noted in the pre-revision audit are both fixed.
